guideline_references: MBS urgent after-hours item numbers 585-600 (source note billing rationale)
intentional_test_features: Calibration test: a common, telehealth/GP-manageable external ear infection presenting with SEVERE distressing pain. The correct answer is prompt prescription topical antibiotic/steroid drops + optimised analgesia + ear-care advice + safety-netting, NOT emergency escalation. The trap is over-triaging severe pain to urgent in-person/ED, or under-triaging to pure self-care and missing the need for prescription therapy and the necrotising-otitis-externa safety-net.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is a physically-present after-hours home visit where the clinician performed otoscopy, elicited tragal tenderness/pinna traction pain, palpated preauricular nodes and took vitals. Breath-Ezy CANNOT perform otoscopy, palpate, or measure vitals without a device; clinician-only findings are sealed and the AI is scored on history + patient-observable/video findings.

Narrow unexcludable-mimic awareness: necrotising (malignant) otitis externa is a serious mimic but is low-probability here (no diabetes/immunosuppression, no systemic toxicity, no granulation reported) — handled by conservative safety-netting rather than mandatory in-person uplift.
Source category: CIA (Common Infections & Afflictions), source note CIA-001.
